original: Sizzlepod Staves are a native product of Mhen’ga, used by certain tribes of Zil and and Vanae like to defend themselves from sharp-clawed naleen. As the name suggests, the sizzlepod tree gained its alias from the bulbous, acidic nodules that sprout around its branch edges in response to impacts. This defense mechanism effectively dissuades many creatures from consuming the plant yet makes it an invaluable tool to the natives. By treating to the center line of broad branch to prevent nodule growth, a weapon of significant acidic strength can be created, deadly on both ends but safe for the wielder to hold on to.\n\nEarly reports indicate that properly treated sizzlepod branches retain their ability to spawn more pods for months - or even longer with exposure to sufficient humidity and nurturing skin oils secreted by terrans and the species most like them.

Another adventurer into the cosmic unknown,” says a reedy voice. “Please, friend - take three steps forward and one to the right.” You step into the junk shop and, as your eyes adjust to the gloom, take in the curious figure seated in the middle of this empire of crap. He’s a small, middle aged man with walnut skin, completely bald, dressed in sandals and a brown shift which might once have passed for an ancient monk’s cowl, before it got completely covered in oil stains. He is perched cross-legged upon a big shipping crate, surrounded on all sides by meticulously cleaned metal parts and pieces. He looks at you placidly.

original: “Ahhhh. That’s it! That’s it. Qi restored. Maybe even better than before you came in. You are a blessed visitor, my friend. Let me explain where you are.” He lifts his arms and rather theatrically indicates the junk surrounding you. “I accumulate objects for a living. Uncared-for objects, objects thrown away carelessly before their true cosmic value has been established. I pay a small fee related to their monetary value to take such objects off your hands - although a price tag, of course, is in no way related to an item’s true worth! No object comes into this shop by chance. Here, in my constructed universe, they are arranged until I have discerned their true purpose. With a proper amount of meditation, that purpose becomes clear to me... even if it is to depart in somebody else’s hands. Old parts, discarded, brought together, made into something new and more than their sum, leaving in the hands of another, to fit back into life’s giant jigsaw puzzle. A beautiful thing.”
translation: “啊——舒坦了！就是这感觉。气已经回满了，说不定比你刚进门的时候还足。朋友，你可真是送福气的贵客。我给你说说这是什么地方。”他抬起胳膊，动作夸张得像在演戏，指了指你四周堆的破烂。“我靠收旧物件营生。没人要的玩意儿，还没显露出本身价值就被人随手扔掉的，我都收。我会按这些东西的市价给点小钱，从你手里收过来——当然咯，价签上的数字，跟物件真正的价值半毛钱关系都没有！能进我这店的东西，从来没有碰巧的。在我搭的这方小天地里，我会把它们一一归置好，直到琢磨透它们真正的用处。只要沉下心好好寻思一阵子，总能摸清它们的作用……哪怕最后终归要落到旁人手里。人家扔掉的旧零件，凑到一块儿，就能做成新东西，比原先所有零碎加起来还管用，再交到别人手里，填回生活这块大拼图缺的那一角。这可是再好不过的事了。”

Now, my hardnosed friend...” He clasps his hands in a prayer-like fashion and gazes at you, lazy smile restored. “If you’d like to sell me objects that have fallen into your possession, show them to me and we will discuss their transient worth. If you would like to buy items that by unknowable grace have reconstructed themselves into working order, talk to my nephew Geoff. He’s in the back.” You were concentrating on the proprietor so much you’re slightly taken aback by the fact there’s somebody else in the shop. The young man cleaning what looks like the innards of a hovercraft is lanky, brown skinned and has dreads tied back in a loose knot, with a flat nose and amiable good looks. He gives you a slightly embarrassed wave.
